theory.intro: Coordinates name the position of a point on a grid as an ordered pair $(x, y)$. To read them, count across, then up.
facts: Coordinates are written $(x, y)$ — **horizontal first**, then vertical.
$x$ is counted **right** from $0$; $y$ is counted **up** from $0$.
The point $(0, 0)$ is the **origin**, where the axes meet.
steps: Drop straight down from the point to read the $x$-axis value.
Look straight across to the $y$-axis to read the $y$ value.
